## Canonicalization rule

This atlas is the canonical derivation stack.

If multiple route notes exist for the same result:

1. this atlas chooses one current authority surface in the package
2. retained/publication docs should point to that canonical surface
3. older route variants belong in work history or route-history notes, not as
   competing authorities

## How to use this atlas

Each row below is a reusable tool or closed subderivation, not a manuscript
headline. The safe reuse question is:

1. what does the tool actually prove?
2. what claim level is safe?
3. what future lanes can build on it?
4. what import class applies?

The `Status / import class` field is part of the toolbox contract. If a row is
not marked `zero-input structural` or `axiom-dependent support`, do not reuse
it as a pure internal theorem without carrying its bridge/import conditions
forward explicitly.

Use the local status/import wording in each row directly. This atlas is a
toolbox surface, not a separate public terminology guide.

For every derived claim retained on the package surface, the load-bearing reusable proof
mechanisms should appear here as separate atlas rows when they can support
other lanes. A claim-level row by itself is not enough if the package depends
on reusable subproofs.

| CKM barred-triangle orthocenter / Euler-line exact closed forms | on the retained protected-`gamma_bar` NLO surface, the classical triangle centers close exactly with `H=(rho_bar,(20+\alpha_s(v))/(24 sqrt(5)))`, apex offset `H-V_3=(0,\alpha_s(v) sqrt(5)/20)`, `G=((28-\alpha_s(v))/72,\sqrt{5}(4-\alpha_s(v))/72)`, `O=(1/2,-\alpha_s(v) sqrt(5)/40)`, and exact Euler relation `H=3G-2O`; the same package identifies the exact slope coincidence `(H_y-V_3,y)/alpha_s(v)=sqrt(5)/20` with the retained N7 apex-angle slope and keeps all center coordinates at at-most-linear `alpha_s(v)` dependence on that surface | retained NLO CKM-structure corollary with finite-`lambda` guardrail | CKM classical-triangle center geometry, Euler-line bookkeeping, N7 slope reuse, and joint-fit comparators | [CKM_BARRED_ORTHOCENTER_EULER_LINE_EXACT_CLOSED_FORM_THEOREM_NOTE_2026-04-25.md](../../CKM_BARRED_ORTHOCENTER_EULER_LINE_EXACT_CLOSED_FORM_THEOREM_NOTE_2026-04-25.md)&nbsp;[audit:unaudited], [CKM_NLO_BARRED_TRIANGLE_PROTECTED_GAMMA_THEOREM_NOTE_2026-04-25.md](../../CKM_NLO_BARRED_TRIANGLE_PROTECTED_GAMMA_THEOREM_NOTE_2026-04-25.md)&nbsp;[audit:unaudited], [CKM_BARRED_TRIANGLE_PYTHAGOREAN_RHO_LAMBDA_SUM_RULE_THEOREM_NOTE_2026-04-25.md](../../CKM_BARRED_TRIANGLE_PYTHAGOREAN_RHO_LAMBDA_SUM_RULE_THEOREM_NOTE_2026-04-25.md)&nbsp;[audit:unaudited], [CKM_BARRED_APEX_ANGLE_EXACT_CLOSED_FORM_THEOREM_NOTE_2026-04-25.md](../../CKM_BARRED_APEX_ANGLE_EXACT_CLOSED_FORM_THEOREM_NOTE_2026-04-25.md)&nbsp;[audit:unaudited] | [frontier_ckm_barred_orthocenter_euler_line_exact_closed_form.py](../../../scripts/frontier_ckm_barred_orthocenter_euler_line_exact_closed_form.py) |
| CKM barred-triangle Pedoe similarity-deficit exact closed form | applying Pedoe's two-triangle similarity inequality to two retained protected-`gamma_bar` NLO triangles collapses the similarity deficit to `PedoeDeficit(alpha_s,alpha_s')=(alpha_s-alpha_s')^2/48`; equivalently `d_Pedoe=|alpha_s-alpha_s'|/(4 sqrt(3))`, so the abstract retained `alpha_s` family is Pedoe-rigid with similarity iff `alpha_s=alpha_s'` | retained NLO CKM-structure corollary with finite-`lambda` guardrail | CKM classical-triangle similarity geometry, alpha_s-parameter metric, selection-rule bookkeeping | [CKM_BARRED_PEDOE_SIMILARITY_DEFICIT_CLOSED_FORM_THEOREM_NOTE_2026-04-25.md](../../CKM_BARRED_PEDOE_SIMILARITY_DEFICIT_CLOSED_FORM_THEOREM_NOTE_2026-04-25.md)&nbsp;[audit:unaudited], [CKM_NLO_BARRED_TRIANGLE_PROTECTED_GAMMA_THEOREM_NOTE_2026-04-25.md](../../CKM_NLO_BARRED_TRIANGLE_PROTECTED_GAMMA_THEOREM_NOTE_2026-04-25.md)&nbsp;[audit:unaudited], [CKM_MAGNITUDES_STRUCTURAL_COUNTS_THEOREM_NOTE_2026-04-25.md](../../CKM_MAGNITUDES_STRUCTURAL_COUNTS_THEOREM_NOTE_2026-04-25.md)&nbsp;[audit:unaudited] | [frontier_ckm_barred_pedoe_similarity_deficit_closed_form.py](../../../scripts/frontier_ckm_barred_pedoe_similarity_deficit_closed_form.py) |
| CKM barred-triangle Napoleon triangles exact closed form | on the retained protected-`gamma_bar` NLO surface, explicit external/internal equilateral-centroid construction gives equilateral Napoleon triangles with side squares `N_outer^2=W_+/6` and `N_inner^2=W_-/6`, product `N_outer^2 N_inner^2=P(alpha_s)/82944`, area product `P(alpha_s)/442368`, LO structural recovery `2/3` and `7/108`, and discriminant `(N_outer^2-N_inner^2)^2=(16/3)Area^2` | retained NLO CKM-structure corollary with finite-`lambda` guardrail | CKM classical-triangle Napoleon geometry, centroid construction, Brocard-polynomial bookkeeping, Jarlskog-area consistency | [CKM_BARRED_NAPOLEON_TRIANGLES_EXACT_CLOSED_FORM_THEOREM_NOTE_2026-04-25.md](../../CKM_BARRED_NAPOLEON_TRIANGLES_EXACT_CLOSED_FORM_THEOREM_NOTE_2026-04-25.md)&nbsp;[audit:unaudited], [CKM_NLO_BARRED_TRIANGLE_PROTECTED_GAMMA_THEOREM_NOTE_2026-04-25.md](../../CKM_NLO_BARRED_TRIANGLE_PROTECTED_GAMMA_THEOREM_NOTE_2026-04-25.md)&nbsp;[audit:unaudited], [CKM_MAGNITUDES_STRUCTURAL_COUNTS_THEOREM_NOTE_2026-04-25.md](../../CKM_MAGNITUDES_STRUCTURAL_COUNTS_THEOREM_NOTE_2026-04-25.md)&nbsp;[audit:unaudited] | [frontier_ckm_barred_napoleon_triangles_exact_closed_form.py](../../../scripts/frontier_ckm_barred_napoleon_triangles_exact_closed_form.py) |

## Atlas rule

Before a subderivation is treated as reusable framework infrastructure:

1. it should have one current authority note on the package surface
2. it should have one runner or explicit validation path
3. its safe claim boundary should be recorded here
4. if older route notes exist, this file should identify the canonical one

If a tool is only visible inside a longer narrative note and not discoverable
here, future lanes are likely to redo work unnecessarily.
